content: UX anti-patterns causing personal finance app abandonment: (1) front-loaded setup — asking for bank account + all categories + goals before showing any value; (2) clutter dashboard — too many numbers visible simultaneously triggers financial anxiety; (3) passive reports without recommendations (showing a pie chart of spending with no 'here is what to do about it'); (4) jargon-heavy language — 3x abandonment rate; (5) forced manual recategorization — any time the app mis-categorizes and forces user to fix it; (6) all-or-nothing syncing — if bank link breaks, app feels dead; (7) time-based budget resets that ignore real income cycles.
